name: CNS Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
business: CNS Pharmaceuticals is a biotechnology company focused on building a high-value pipeline in neurology and oncology, with a new corporate strategy announced March 11, 2026. The company is exploring out-licensing opportunities for its legacy assets TPI 287 and Berubicin, which are investigational compounds for glioblastoma multiforme. As of December 31, 2025, the company had no approved products and had not generated any revenue from product sales.

exposures.sentence: CNS Pharmaceuticals, Inc. ( CNS, the Company, we, us, or our ) is a biotechnology company focused on developing innovative therapies for serious diseases in neurology and oncology.
Our new strategic focus on neurology and oncology positions us in two of the largest, fastest-growing, and most scientifically dynamic sectors in biopharmaceuticals.
Our new strategic focus on neurology and oncology positions us in two of the largest, fastest-growing, and most scientifically dynamic sectors in biopharmaceuticals.

The Company s prior development programs centered on two investigational compounds, TPI 287 and Berubicin, both of which were designed to penetrate the blood-brain barrier and target cancers of the central nervous system such as GBM.

catalysts.sentence: On March 11, 2026, we announced a new corporate growth strategy focused on building a high-value pipeline in neurology and oncology.
On January 1, 2026, the Company appointed Rami Levin, MBA, as President & Chief Executive Officer.

catalysts.event: Appointment of new executive team (CBO, CFO, CMO, CTO)
catalysts.sentence: This executive team was assembled to execute on the findings and recommendations of the Strategic Analysis and develop and execute a new corporate strategy.

catalysts.event: Topline results from Berubicin Phase 2 trial announced
catalysts.sentence: In March 2025, the Company announced topline results from the primary analysis of the trial.

tamClaims.claim: Global neurological therapeutics market exceeds $138 billion
tamClaims.sentence: The global neurological therapeutics market exceeds $138 billion while the global oncology therapeutics market is projected to reach $400 billion in the next decade.

tamClaims.claim: Global oncology therapeutics market projected to reach $400 billion in the next decade
tamClaims.sentence: The global neurological therapeutics market exceeds $138 billion while the global oncology therapeutics market is projected to reach $400 billion in the next decade.

tamClaims.claim: Neurological disorders affect more than one billion people globally
tamClaims.sentence: According to WHO, neurological disorders affect more than one billion people globally and are the leading cause of disability-adjusted life years ( DALYs ) when all conditions are aggregated across the spectrum of central nervous system ( CNS ) disease.

tamClaims.claim: Cancer accounts for approximately 10 million deaths per year
tamClaims.sentence: According to the WHO, cancer accounts for approximately 10 million deaths per year, which accounted for nearly one in six deaths globally.

tamClaims.claim: In 2022, nearly 20 million new cancer cases and approximately 9.7 million cancer-related deaths worldwide
tamClaims.sentence: In 2022, the International Agency for Research on Cancer ( IARC ) recorded nearly 20 million new cancer cases and approximately 9.7 million cancer-related deaths worldwide.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 2,041,910 new cancer cases will be diagnosed in the US in 2025, with approximately 618,120 deaths
tamClaims.sentence: In the United States alone, the National Cancer Institute ( NCI ) estimated that approximately 2,041,910 new cancer cases will be diagnosed in 2025, with approximately 618,120 deaths attributable to the disease.
